capture.content:
# Model string references ("app_label.ModelName") — case-sensitivity and where they live

- Relational fields store lazy references to models as strings of the form `app_label.ModelName`. App labels ARE case-sensitive (mixed-case app labels like `DJ_RegLogin` are legal); only the model-name half may be lowercased for normalization. Lowercasing the whole string (e.g. `self.remote_field.model.lower()`) silently breaks apps with mixed-case labels — the app registry then reports the (lowercased) app as "not installed" during makemigrations/migrate.
- Key construction point: `ForeignObject.deconstruct()` in `django/db/models/fields/related.py` (~line 584) builds the string form of `to` for migrations. This is where reference-normalization bugs originate.
- Downstream consumers of these strings (they resolve, not create, references — usually NOT where the fix belongs): `django/db/migrations/state.py`, `django/db/migrations/autodetector.py`, `django/db/migrations/operations/utils.py` (resolve_relation), `django/db/models/options.py`, and the app registry's lazy-reference check that raises `ValueError: ... was declared with a lazy reference to ... but app ... isn't installed`.

## Localization lesson
For bugs where a model/app reference string comes out wrong, fix the single point where the string is CONSTRUCTED (deconstruct/serialization), not the many places it is consumed. Real fix for the mixed-case app-label makemigrations crash touched only `django/db/models/fields/related.py`; the agent's 5-file candidate list (autodetector, state, options, operations/utils) was 4 files too wide.
